entities.name: SWEET POTATO
entities.description: An important root crop with significant nutritional and economic value in sub-Saharan countries.

entities.name: SOUTH AFRICA
entities.description: A sub-Saharan country where sweet potato badnaviruses were reported for the first time.

entities.name: SWEET POTATO BADNAVIRUS A
entities.description: A pararetrovirus detected in sweet potato plants in South Africa, sharing 100% nucleotide identity with a Chinese isolate.

entities.name: SWEET POTATO BADNAVIRUS B
entities.description: A pararetrovirus detected in sweet potato plants in South Africa, sharing 99% nucleotide identity with a Spanish isolate.

entities.name: SMALL RNA DEEP SEQUENCING
entities.description: A technique used for the discovery and detection of sweet potato badnaviruses.

entities.name: TANZANIA
entities.description: A country where sweet potato badnaviruses were previously detected.

entities.name: HONDURAS
entities.description: A country where sweet potato badnaviruses were previously detected.

entities.name: GUATEMALA
entities.description: A country where sweet potato badnaviruses were previously detected.

entities.name: BADNAVIRUSES
entities.description: Pararetroviruses of the family Caulimoviridae with circular double-stranded DNA genomes.

entities.name: CAULIMOVIRIDAE
entities.description: The family of pararetroviruses to which badnaviruses belong.

entities.name: CIRCULAR DOUBLE-STRANDED DNA GENOMES
entities.description: The genomic structure of badnaviruses, ranging between 7.2 and 8.5 kb in size.

entities.name: EASTERN CAPE PROVINCE
entities.description: A province in South Africa where symptomatic sweet potato plants were collected.

entities.name: WESTERN CAPE PROVINCE
entities.description: A province in South Africa where symptomatic sweet potato plants were collected.

entities.name: LEAF CURLING
entities.description: A symptom exhibited by sweet potato plants infected with badnaviruses.

entities.name: CHLOROTIC SPOTS
entities.description: A symptom exhibited by sweet potato plants infected with badnaviruses.

entities.name: CHLOROTIC SPOTS WITH PURPLE RINGS
entities.description: A symptom exhibited by sweet potato plants infected with badnaviruses.

entities.name: SANGER SEQUENCING
entities.description: A method used to confirm the identity of the detected badnaviruses.

entities.name: POLYMERASE CHAIN REACTION
entities.description: A method used to amplify virus-specific primers for identification.

entities.name: POLYPROTEIN GENE
entities.description: A gene targeted by primers to identify SWEET POTATO BADNAVIRUS A.

entities.name: ORF3B GENE
entities.description: A gene targeted by primers to identify SWEET POTATO BADNAVIRUS B.

entities.name: CHINA
entities.description: Country from which a 100% identical isolate of SWEET POTATO BADNAVIRUS A originated.

entities.name: SPAIN
entities.description: Country from which a 99% identical isolate of SWEET POTATO BADNAVIRUS B originated.

entities.name: SWEET POTATO DISEASE
entities.description: Diseases in sweet potato that badnaviruses are known to trigger or contribute to.
